title: HTML Minifier
shortDescription: Minify HTML in the browser to shrink markup size without sending code anywhere.
metaDescription: Minify HTML in your browser to reduce file size, strip unnecessary whitespace, and keep markup ready for deployment.
intro: Paste HTML and get a smaller, deployment-ready version with extra whitespace, line breaks, and optional comments removed.
overview:
This HTML minifier compresses markup while keeping the document structure intact. It is useful when you need cleaner output for templates, static pages, snippets, or exported HTML that includes editor spacing.

Because it runs in the browser, your code stays local while it is processed. You can review the minified result before copying it into a build, a CMS, or a page template, which makes it easier to catch spacing-sensitive sections and confirm whether comments should stay or go.
howItWorks: Paste your HTML into the input field.
Choose whether comments should be removed.
Run minification to compress the markup.
Copy the minified HTML and use it in your project.

useCases.title: Deployment build output
useCases.description: Shrink page markup before shipping a release so the final HTML is leaner and easier to transfer through build steps.

useCases.title: Exported template cleanup
useCases.description: Remove editor spacing and comment blocks from HTML exported by design tools, site builders, or generators.

useCases.title: Static page publishing
useCases.description: Prepare hand-written landing page HTML for upload when you want a tighter source file.

useCases.title: Email template cleanup
useCases.description: Compress HTML fragments used in transactional or marketing email templates where every character matters.

useCases.title: Reviewing markup changes
useCases.description: Compare how much whitespace and optional comments affect the size of a specific document or snippet.

examples.title: Collapse nested spacing
examples.description: Removes line breaks and repeated spaces while keeping the tag structure unchanged.

examples.title: Keep content, drop comments
examples.description: Removes HTML comments when the comment option is enabled.

examples.title: Trim repeated indentation in a list
examples.description: Useful for copied snippets that contain heavy indentation from an editor or formatter.

faq.question: Will this tool preserve my tags and attributes exactly?
faq.answer: It keeps the HTML structure intact, but it may normalize whitespace around text nodes and remove comments if you choose that option.

faq.question: When should I avoid minifying a fragment?
faq.answer: Avoid it for content that depends on literal spacing inside text nodes, preformatted blocks, or markup where whitespace is part of the visible output.

faq.question: Why does the output look different after minification?
faq.answer: The tool removes line breaks and compresses repeated spaces, so the source becomes denser even when the rendered page should remain the same.

faq.question: Can comments be kept in the result?
faq.answer: Yes. If comment removal is disabled, the tool leaves HTML comments in place.

seo.title: HTML Minifier | Compress HTML
seo.description: Minify HTML in your browser to reduce file size, strip unnecessary whitespace, and keep markup ready for deployment.
